item.title: Honey, I Shrunk the MTF
item.content:encoded:
Private Lang, Private Pimm

I see what you did there.

Overall, this is goofy and requires some serious suspension of disbelief, but I found it enjoyable nonetheless. +1 because I'm a sucker for camp.

+ Problems with shrinking
- But I'm sure you already knew that

All units (both human and vehicular) of SCP-3198 have been reduced dramatically to a ratio of approximately 1:64 of their original size with a similar reduction in mass.

Let's assume a height of six feet (72 inches), and a weight of 180 lbs. Some quick division tells us that such a person in this situation would be only one and an eighth inches tall, but nearly three pounds in weight. At such a density, I find it unlikely that anything the MTF members come across would be able to do them significant harm (with the exception of poisons like the pesticide spray).

Even falling from the truck as it's picked up by a "giant" wouldn't be enough to kill them. Yes, proportionally, it's like falling from a tall building. But that's not how you calculate the force from a fall. It all comes down to mass and acceleration from gravity. I thought about going into the math for this, but for the sake of brevity, let it suffice to say that two objects of any size, with the same mass, falling from the same height, will hit the ground with the same amount of force. So a normal sized human and a shrunken human with the same mass, falling from the height of one meter, would experience the same amount of force. A shrunken human with diminished mass falling from one meter would experience diminished force by comparison. And considering the density of the shrunken humans in this situation, they'd be more than tough enough to survive that fall.

Related to that, assuming that the helicopter is still capable of flight at that density, it would chop through any birds that tried to attack it. Unless seagulls have wire cutters for beaks, they're not breaking any part of that chopper. Maybe if one fell on top of it, kamikaze style, it could stop up the blades enough to make it fall. But then it's still only actually falling three or four storeys. I'm not super sure on the survivability here, but my gut says it's more than you might first imagine.

Finally, and perhaps most significantly to the plot, there's actually a legitimate reason why the tiny MTF would have difficulty communicating with the Foundation and perceiving the world around them. This idea was introduced to me years ago in a book called "The Science of Superheroes", and it's a great read that I'd highly recommend. Basically, if a human shrunk down to the size of a bug, their eyes would be too small to take in light the way they're supposed to. The book also discussed how hearing would be affected, but I don't remember what it said. The end result though is that the MTF actually wouldn't be able to effectively communicate with the Foundation, or possibly even read the word "Jenga" written in (relatively) large letters. So you actually got that part right!… Except that they would have similar difficulty perceiving themselves and each other, and would definitely not be able to read text messages, or tiny words on paper.

Oh yeah, and that pesky square-cube law means that they'd all lack the surface area required to release body heat, so they'd develop a fever and die in… I dunno, a few minutes?

item.title: Re: Partial rewrite may be in the works
item.content:encoded: Can I see it? Like a link? I might be able to finish it (If it isn't already)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree, the Foundation should have been able to communicate with them by now. However, I think I've come up with a fairly simple rewrite to make this whole "communication barrier" thing work. What if some of the higher ups in the miniature task force, due to years of working to contain extremely dangerous SCPs (including reality altering entities), have become almost delusional, refusing to accept the evidence that the task force was shrunk? Instead, they're actively covering up evidence (such as "successful" communication with the rest of the Foundation) and hiding it from their subordinates in order to prevent them from being "tricked" by the giants.

I think incorporating the idea of some sort of delusional conspiracy and coverup on the part of some of the members of SCP-3198 would work really well. Some members of the task force are bound to get suspicious of the coverup, but whenever someone knows too much, they end up dying in an "accident" or "killed by giants".

Also, I understand them being untrusting, given that everything wants to nibble on their tasty bits, but considering only one of the "giants" has actively tried to harm them, their extreme hostility is odd. This can be rewritten as evidence of some sort of indoctrination on the part of the delusional members of the task force. When you consider exploration logs of other task force teams involving other dangerous entities, much of what they do revolves around observation to learn about potential threats.

PS
I'm not able to do the rewrite. High school sucked out all my creative juices.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure about 061, but I'm pretty sure what happened with 999 was that 682 somehow adapted to release the euphoria given to it by 999, in order to remove it's effects. After all, 999 needed skin contact for it's euphoria effect, while 682 released a large burst of euphoria as a wave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm echoing other sentiments on this - the communication barrier doesn't make sense as is. Nothing about what happened seems to lead to the conclusion that they can't understand speech or writing suddenly - plus, it makes no sense that the Foundation can understand them but they can't understand the Foundation. (Also, their speech shouldn't be sped up - unless their time experience is also sped up, in which case they should probably be dead by now of old age.)

I recognize that the central conceit - they think they're in some sort of alternate universe or something and unable to make sense of it - is rather important to the hostility on display. But that means one of two things: either you need to ditch the hostility or you need to change the underlying factors to fit the conceit. So two possible directions - one, make it so they can communicate with the Foundation and help into their growing despair at the situation, or two, change the way they get shifted, make them mutually unintelligible with the Foundation, and focus on the angle of trying to figure out how to establish some kind of communication and the troubles they've faced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhh this was kind of made by 682 rather than the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would it make sense for "foundation-made" to be in the tags?

(Sorry, I meant for this to be a new post, not a direct reply.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In regards to 682 using 1056's ability, this is something that 682 has done before with 999 and 061 which is why I've done it here though I do still understand that this does take a bit of a liberty with 682.

As for the rest, I agree and it is something on the back of my mind to try and rewrite at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It irks me that 682 adopted the abilities of the other anomaly when it simply adapts to survive instead. Also, I felt communication could be possible in a few ways such as LED lights, create a letter that too be shrunk and sent, or communicate with Morse code on the radio than speech. I just feel a way to communicate would already be achieved. Also, I feel they would definitely have prevented the deaths of the guys at the end.

I really do like the document detailing their perspective. It is rather amusing.

item.title: Ever heard of Occam's Razor?
item.content:encoded:
Well the simplest answer is typically the true answer according to Occam's Razor.
So whats the simplest solution to the problem of these guys? Attempt to shrink someone again and lead them to the whereabouts of the group so they can tell the MTF about what happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot. But the other day I read an article about a giant funnel which looks like a cylinder when you look through it. Then when you drop something through it you can shrink or grow the object by a factor of 12. I can see the Foundation doing something with the funnel SCP to shrink a squad of agents to try to make contact with the MTF and explain the situation to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh. This being the result of some fuckery with 682 feels like a throwback to older articles in a less than positive way. Don't get me wrong, there's some amusing bits to it, but ultimately the communication barrier just feels contrived (I understand that you were going for their lack of understanding being anomalous - it still feels contrived - even more so in some aspects). Ultimately while it held my attention to the end, I left the article feeling kinda annoyed and frustrated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing what others have said, it seems like the Foundation should be able to communicate with them and even if they weren't the task force ought to be able to work out that they've been shrunk. They're in a giant building with giant people and giant birds and giant insects, it just seems the most logical conclusion and it is weird that it's not the conclusion they came to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah it's something I'm either going to make clearer or rewrite but they basically just can't understand any communication. Writing to them looks like weird symbols.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one complaint here is that I feel like it wouldn't be too hard to get a message to them. They can still read, right? The Foundation can always just write out what you want to say to them and shrink it down significantly, explaining the full situation… It might not fully make them trust them, but it probably would at the very least help them start to establish relations, right? Am I missing something here?

item.title: Partial rewrite may be in the works
item.content:encoded:
Update 18/02/2017

I am currently considering doing a rewrite of this. I have got something on the sandbox however this SCP isn't doing too badly. If I feel it needs it, I will go through with a rewrite but for now I will leave it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though there are some good parts here, stuff like "fucking demonic screaming" and the 682 crosstest don't impress me that much.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not to mention that written materials should be pretty easy to read. A Jenga block blown up to 64x its normal size would have letters on it approximately 1m high. I can read letters that are 1m high. Why can't the task force read the equivalent size letters on the blocks?

One of the other things that bugs me: I can't imagine that the MTF is so slow on the uptake that they wouldn't have realised what was going on if they knew that 682 was being cross-tested with 1056. But if they didn't know, that reflects awfully on Foundation Command that is fully aware of 682's adaptability, but didn't think to warn the containment team of what it might be adapting to?

Speaking of which, 682 typically adapts to and regenerates from environmental stresses - it doesn't tend to take on their properties. As such, I struggled with the premise that 682 was able to replicate the effect of 1056, but more effectively and on a higher scale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that seems to be the main complaint. I may add something where maybe they have contained a few of them and managed to talk to communicate with them somehow but they're a bit wary of releasing them again to talk to the others as the others are extremely paranoid and will likely think it's a trick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept, but I kind of can't buy that the Foundation is having a hard time communicating with them. It seems like there should be a simple mathematical conversion to achieve the proper frequency needed to both understand them and be understandable to them. Whether or not they want to communicate or acknowledge what the Foundation is saying is another matter, but I'd think something would get through. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, accidently posted multiple of the same comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought the first image wasn't great. If you remove the word "demonic" from "fucking demonic screaming" I think it sounds more natural. Also, I think it would be good to contain a note that said "It is being investigated whether SCP-3198 is an anomaly in itself or simply the product of one." Otherwise, it's a fun Gulliver's Travels-esque SCP that I think you wrote pretty well. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ;n; This scp is so innocent and a very simple idea. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok so I did post the yesterday but I got very mixed feedback on this so I decided to take it down. Since then I've had a lot of people say I should put it up again as they liked it. I'm going to post this again and just let the voters decide on this one, upvote/downvote, whatever you choose, for those that downvoted this before, please feel free to do so again, this is not me trying to get around the downvotes more I just made a bit of a snap decision. Feel free to leave your feedback again if you choose.

Images were taken by me except the IRL vehicle which came from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:M-ATV-Overall_022.jpg

Thank you for everyone who has helped with the feedback on this.
